title: STANDARD FOR SWEETENED CONDENSED MILK CXS 282-1971

sections.section: STANDARD FOR SWEETENED CONDENSED MILK CXS 282-1971
sections.summary: Document title and history: Standard for Sweetened Condensed Milk (CXS 282-1971), adopted 1971 and subsequently revised in 1999 and amended in 2010, 2018, 2022 and 2023.
Notes that amendments adopted in December 2022 at CAC45 changed Section 7.5 on labelling of non-retail containers.
States that amendments from December 2023 at CAC46 moved and aligned the food additives provisions with the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, CXS 192-1995).
Scope: this standard applies to sweetened condensed milk intended for direct consumption or further processing, as described in Section 2.

Defines sweetened condensed milk as milk concentrated by partial removal of water with added sugar (or equivalent process) and allows fat/protein adjustment only insofar as it meets Section 3 compositional requirements without altering the whey protein to casein ratio.

Covers essential composition and quality factors: lists permitted raw materials and protein‑adjustment products, permitted ingredients, and composition requirements (see 3.3) including product-class specific minima/maxima and notes sugar levels are controlled by GMP to ensure keeping quality and avoid crystallization.

Permits milk and milk powders, cream and cream powders, milkfat products, and specifically allows milk retentate, milk permeate and lactose for protein adjustment (with brief definitions of retentate and permeate).
Permitted ingredients are potable water, sugar (generally sucrose but combinations with other sugars consistent with GMP are allowed) and sodium chloride.

Specifies compositional minima/maxima for product classes: e.g. standard sweetened condensed milk minimum milkfat 8% and milk solids 28% m/m, skimmed maximum milkfat 1% and minimum solids 24% m/m, partly skimmed 1–8% milkfat with specified solids minima, high‑fat minimum milkfat 16% etc., and states sugar levels are limited by GMP to safeguard shelf life and prevent crystallization.

Restricts food additives to those functional classes and specific substances indicated as technologically justified in the GSFA (CXS 192-1995) for condensed milk (category 01.3.1) and permits certain acidity regulators, emulsifiers, firming agents, stabilizers and thickeners as listed.

Requires the product and the milk used in its manufacture to comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in CXS 193-1995 and with Codex maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides applicable to milk.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Provides specific labelling provisions supplementing general rules: prescribes permitted product names/variants, requires declaration of milkfat and milk protein where omission would mislead (with acceptable formats), exempts milk products used only for protein adjustment from ingredient listing, and directs non‑retail container labelling to CXS 346-2021.

Specifies allowed product names—Sweetened condensed milk; Sweetened condensed skimmed milk; Sweetened condensed partly skimmed milk; Sweetened condensed high‑fat milk—and allows the designation 'sweetened condensed semi‑skimmed milk' for partly skimmed product when milkfat is 4.0–4.5% and minimum milk solids are 28% m/m.

If omission would mislead the consumer, milk protein content must be declared for sale to the final consumer in a manner acceptable in the country of sale either as a percentage by mass or volume or as grams per serving provided the number of servings is stated.
Notwithstanding the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), milk products used solely for protein adjustment are exempt from declaration in the ingredients list.
Labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
For compliance checking with this standard, the relevant methods of analysis and sampling in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) shall be used.
Bibliographic notes listing referenced Codex standards and codes of practice (not a substantive regulatory provision).
